replies.author: @frocat5163
replies.text:
@GIBBO4182 
Fun Fact: Many/Most of the words the British claim/complain have been "Americanized" are actually the original, or closer to the original, than modern British spellings/pronunciations.

This is observed in many colonies. It's called 'Colonial Lag', and while most linguists consider it an oversimplification of how language evolves, it's also easily observed by comparing Australian and American English to modern British English. Both descended from British English at the time the colonies experienced a surge in immigration, but Australian English proceeded from late-18th century English, while American English proceeded from mid-17th century English. That being said, this almost certainly isn't the case for 'rumor / rumour', though. It's possible, maybe even probable, that words like rumour/rumor were being spelled colloquially without the 'u' by the late 18th century in the US/American Colonies, but educated individuals were more likely to use the '-our' ending.

For English words ending in '-our' and '-or' that are ultimately derived from Latin (generally through French), the Latin spelling was almost always '-or'. In Webster's 1828 dictionary, most words ending '-our' were simplified to the original Latin '-or'. So, one could argue that the American versions of those words are more accurate since they align with the Latin spelling. Ultimately, though, it's likely that at least part of Webster's decision to change the spelling was to create more separation between the US and Britain by making American English distinct.

I've always found the evolution of American English from British English to be fascinating for some reason.

replies.author: @jayp1286
replies.text:
​@MooKyTigYou cant just say it was not good you have to say how. And let me tell you. I played legendary and andromeda right after when legendary came out. And andromeda was great. Great new characters. Made driving on the surface far more relevant than its ever been. I liked the exploratory feel of being in a universe nobody knew as opposed to being the one person learning all the lore in the first 3. I like the story element of the blended AI and was looking forward to where it was going. I liked the platforming elements like how you had to find stuff on the very first jungle.planet (where you couldnt drive yet). I liked the maps. I liked the worlds. I like how when you did things the planets themselves could change a little. I liked the super kaiju boss fights. 

What fucking game where you playing? Where you playing "im playing a gamr but Im also on social media and doing 5 other things and not paying 100% attention to the game" because i find a lot of people do that and then they say something was trash and really whats trash is their inability to enjoy things and do things properly. 

All i heard is that they had to patch some graphics on the aliens because they looked funny at first. This is the one issue i heard about. So Cyberpunk gets a second chance after it fixes its major release bugs but Andromeda doesnt?

author: @EmpyreanDreamer
text:
I thought it wasn't as bad as the GoT ending as well but after looking into this theory I think I have to elevate the show to GoT levels of bad. The odds of the theory being true are pretty much zero but the fact that its so compelling and difficult to argue against is truly hilarious. It really shows the outstanding incompetence and lack of attention to detail which went into this season. So many things that you can take as very specific references or outright hits but in reality have absolutely no real purpose, its just absolute lack of continuity and it all combines together to create some amazing coincidences which are infinitely more compelling than the season itself.

At least with GoT it was just awful. This season being made with such incompetence that it actually convinces you its a fake out and they're gonna drop the real finale is so much worse. The answers the Duffer brothers have been giving in interviews make it even worse, its like they're trying to annoy people which just makes everybody more convinced the theory is true. What a sad ending to a great show.

author: @sebastienbolduc5654
text:
The person pointing out that the doors in the basement were flipped the other way around, I have seen this with The Walking Dead. Sometimes it's just laziness on behalf of the production crew and management. TWD did some web episodes. One of them was about the nurse who took care of Rick Grimes after the outbreak when he was abandoned in the hospital. The colours on the wall are different. The bullet holes in the wall beside the, "Don't Open, Dead Inside" door are not there, etc. Even some of the furnishing is different or misplaced. I thought this was a clue that the whole show was Rick's mind in a coma. That the end of TWD would be Rick waking up and realizing that it was all in his head. Nope!!! It was just pure laziness and budget cuts by AMC while they pocketed more of that production money into their own pockets. 

I also noticed that Mrs Wheeler did not have scars when she called them for dinner. The whole show could be that the entire story was part of a D&D game that the children made up, based characters on themselves and people that they know, and it was all make-believe. Now that they are entering into adulthood, symbolized by the younger children playing D&D while the older children walked away, the story came to an end. Mike then starts creating a D&D styled game called, "Stranger Things." The word, "Fantasy" written on Mike's cover for his, "Fantasy Adventure Game," was probably put there to clue the audience to the fact that it was a story that the children made up.

text: Honestly if I was The Duffer Brothers I would capitalize off this Theory and retcon the ending and write something better this is actually genius and actually this could be a way they could get out of it
